rows.source_quote: Q1 2024 call (Apr 23 2024): Musk committed to FSD V12 rollout to broader fleet within months.
rows.note: Milestone HIT — V12 reached fleet by Q3 2024.

rows.metric: Vehicle delivery growth notably lower than 2023
rows.source_quote: Q4 2023 call (Jan 24 2024): "2024 vehicle volume growth rate may be notably lower than the growth rate achieved in 2023."
rows.note: Directional forecast was correct — 2024 deliveries flat to down vs 2023.

rows.source_quote: Q2 2024 call (Jul 23 2024): Robotaxi unveil postponed to October 10 2024.
Q3 2024 call (Oct 23 2024): Musk projected 20-30% vehicle delivery growth in 2025.
rows.note: Massive MISS — 2025 deliveries declined ~1% YoY vs +20-30% guide.

rows.metric: Unsupervised FSD paid ride-hail launch in Austin June 2025
rows.source_quote: Q4 2024 call (Jan 29 2025): paid autonomous ride-hail to launch in Austin June 2025.
rows.note: Milestone HIT — Robotaxi service in Austin June 22, 2025.

rows.source_quote: Q4 2024 call (Jan 29 2025): "target a production goal of 10,000 Optimus units in 2025."
rows.note: Massive MISS — Optimus production fell far short of 10K target.

rows.source_quote: Q1 2025 call (Apr 22 2025): Cybercab volume production targeted for 2026.
rows.note: As of May 2026, Cybercab volume production has slipped — prototypes only.

rows.metric: Affordable Model Y variant production H2 2025
rows.actual: Began production late 2025 at lower volume
rows.source_quote: Q2 2025 call (Jul 23 2025): more affordable Model Y variant to begin production H2 2025.
rows.note: Partial credit — production started but at lower volumes than promised.

scoring_methodology: Strict: BEAT = actual above HIGH end of guidance (1.0); HIT = within range (0.5); MISS = below LOW end (0.0). Reported MCS = weighted average.
name: Tesla, Inc.
